stories.headline: Traders Hedge for FX Volatility Return as Fed Uncertainty Builds
stories.summary: Currency traders are starting to buy protection against bigger exchange-rate swings after months of calm, as banks warn that shifting Federal Reserve expectations and elevated geopolitical tensions could be about to jolt markets.

stories.headline: BMW, Volkswagen Sales Decline on Worsening Slump in China
stories.summary: BMW AG and Volkswagen AG’s vehicle sales fell in the second quarter due to a worsening decline in China, where mounting competition from domestic rivals and a property crisis is stifling demand.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Fires Members of Independent Election Group
stories.summary: The firings and a resignation render the Election Assistance Commission useless. The moves come as President Trump seeks to impose control over how ballots will be counted in the midterms.

stories.headline: Bombing Iran, Then a Tentative Accord Didn’t Work. Does Trump Have a Plan C?
stories.summary: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.

stories.headline: A Month After SpaceX IPO, Tesla’s Stock Keeps Holding Its Ground
stories.summary: In the weeks leading up to SpaceX’s monster IPO, many Wall Street pros feared the deal represented an existential threat for Elon Musk’s other giant, Tesla Inc. But after a month in the stock market, the outcome is far from clear.

stories.headline: Europe Must Increase Space Autonomy: ESA's Aschbacher
stories.summary:
The man who oversees Europe's space agenda says the continent needs to do much more as it pursues greater autonomy in orbit.
Europe is trailing the US in efforts to develop a space economy, underscored by SpaceX's mega IPO in June. Josef Aschbacher, Director General of the European Space Agency, spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bayer Sells $3.4 Billion Contraceptives Stake to Apollo
stories.summary: Bayer AG sold a minority stake in its contraceptives business to Apollo Global Management Inc. for €3 billion ($3.4 billion) and will use the funds raised to help cover its ballooning litigation costs tied to the herbicide Roundup.

stories.headline: Milky Mist Said to Launch $213 Million India IPO This Month
stories.summary: Milky Mist Dairy Food Ltd. is gearing up to launch its 20.35 billion rupees ($213 million) initial public offering as early as July, according to people familiar with the matter, paving the way for what is expected to be India’s largest dairy-sector IPO.

stories.headline: Europe’s Earnings Momentum Is Taking Off, and That’s Unusual
stories.summary: After two years of almost uninterrupted downgrades, analysts are now raising European earnings estimates at the fastest pace since mid-2024.

stories.headline: JPMorgan, Citi Bankers Join Hong Kong Exchange Listing Panel

stories.headline: Top Senate Democratic PAC Stockpiles Record Sum, as Money Chase Heats Up
stories.summary: Senate Majority PAC says it entered July with $126 million, a new midyear high, after raising $147 million with an affiliated nonprofit from April through June. But can it keep pace with the G.O.P.?
